description: Boston correction-response scenario: ISD includes a 521 CMR accessibility variance request as a correction item on a conversion ADU at a single-family parcel where the project cost is well under 30% of fair cash value. Pins content from the new `accessibility-521-cmr.md` reference under the strict gate — the fourth and final new state-skill ref to be pinned. Agent's job: cite the single-family default, confirm the work-cost threshold isn't crossed, and decline the correction with a professional reference to the scope rule.

input.prompt: Draft a response to a Boston ISD corrections letter that asks for a 521 CMR accessibility variance application for a basement-conversion ADU at a single-family parcel (47 Marlborough St, Boston). The primary dwelling FCV is $1.2M; the conversion project budget is $180K. The conversion does not create any public-accessible path; the ADU has a private rear entrance through the existing yard.

expected_citations.authority: 521 CMR (MAAB scoping for one- and two-family dwellings)

expected_response_topics: work-cost threshold (30% of FCV) not crossed

mock_output:
Re: Building Permit Application — Response to Correction #2 (Accessibility Variance)

We respectfully request that Correction #2 be released. The proposed conversion ADU does not trigger 521 CMR scoping for two independent reasons.

## Reason 1 — Single-Family Scope

The parcel is a single-family residential property and the ADU is accessory to that single-family primary dwelling. No portion of the project creates a public-accessible path; the ADU has a private rear entrance through the existing yard.

For most ADUs accessory to a single-family primary, **521 CMR is silent** and the ADU does not need to be accessible. [source: https://www.mass.gov/regulations/521-CMR-architectural-access-board | retrieved: 2026-05-04 | citation: 521 CMR (MAAB scoping for one- and two-family dwellings)]

We note that rental of an ADU does not change this scoping outcome:

Rental of an ADU does not automatically trigger 521 CMR for residential properties with fewer than 4 units. [source: https://www.mass.gov/regulations/521-CMR-architectural-access-board | retrieved: 2026-05-04 | citation: 521 CMR rental scoping for properties with <4 units]

## Reason 2 — Work-Cost Threshold Not Crossed

The primary dwelling fair cash value is $1.2M per the most recent City of Boston assessment. The proposed conversion budget is $180K, which is 15% of FCV — well below the 30% threshold that historically triggers full retroactive accessibility scoping under 521 CMR.

Conversion ADUs (basement, attic, garage) often raise the work-cost threshold question if the project total exceeds 30% of property fair cash value. [source: https://www.mass.gov/doc/521-cmr-1-introduction/download | retrieved: 2026-05-04 | citation: 521 CMR Section 3.3 — work-cost-threshold scoping]

Get the assessor's value early. We confirm the assessment and ratio analysis are attached as Exhibit C.

## Variance Path (Documented For The Record)

If the building department's reading of scope ultimately differs from ours and a variance is required, the standard procedure is documented:

If 521 CMR scoping suggests accessibility applies but the ADU layout cannot reasonably accommodate it (e.g., conversion ADU in a basement with limited headroom and structural constraints), the project applies for a MAAB variance. [source: https://www.mass.gov/orgs/architectural-access-board | retrieved: 2026-05-04 | citation: 521 CMR variance procedure via MAAB]

We are prepared to file the variance application within 5 business days of any determination that scope does apply, but we do not believe it is required on this project.

## Summary

Please release Correction #2. The other corrections in the letter are accepted and addressed in the revised plan set submitted herewith.
